title: thelesperma subnudum var. marginatum
intro: Thelesperma subnudum var. marginatum, also known as Naked Goldenrod, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly by Native American tribes, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Thelesperma subnudum var. marginatum include relief from minor skin irritations, wounds, and sore throats, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. Traditionally, the plant has been used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or oil to harness its medicinal properties.
